name: jwt-auth
description:
Use when implementing JWT authentication in FastAPI or Python projects.
Triggers for: token generation, verification middleware, current user extraction,
access token creation, token decoding, or role-based auth.
NOT for: OAuth2 provider setup, OpenID Connect, or non-Python backends.
capabilities: Generate secure JWT access tokens with configurable expiry using HS256 algorithm
Create FastAPI dependencies for token verification and user extraction
Implement role-based access control (RBAC) with RoleChecker
Validate JWT structure, expiration claims, and token blacklisting
Configure JWT secret keys and algorithm settings via environment variables
Extract user identity from JWT payload claims
author: Awais68
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing JWT authentication patterns and a SKILL.md validation script. The verify.py script only reads and validates its own SKILL.md file using standard Python file I/O and regex operations. No network calls, no external command execution, no credential access. All 58 static findings are false positives: regex functions (re.match/re.search) were misclassified as cryptographic algorithms, markdown inline code backticks were misclassified as shell execution, and environment variable documentation was misclassified as credential access.
valueStatement: Building secure JWT authentication requires understanding token creation, verification, and role-based access control patterns. This skill provides expert implementations and workflows for adding authentication to FastAPI and Python applications with minimal security risks.
limitations: Does not implement OAuth2 provider or OpenID Connect flows
Frontend token storage and refresh logic handled by separate auth-integration skill
Does not include database integration for user persistence

useCases.title: Add auth to FastAPI APIs
useCases.description: Protect endpoints with JWT bearer token validation and extract user identity from tokens

useCases.title: Build secure API backends
useCases.description: Implement authentication layer that integrates with frontend auth context for complete auth flow

useCases.title: Enforce role-based access
useCases.description: Restrict endpoint access based on user roles with minimal configuration overhead

promptTemplates.title: Basic token generation
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to create a JWT access token
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a JWT access token with subject user@example.com and 30 minute expiry using HS256 algorithm

promptTemplates.title: Protect endpoint
promptTemplates.prompt: Add JWT authentication to my FastAPI endpoint using HTTPBearer dependency and get_current_user

promptTemplates.title: Role-based access
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a RoleChecker that allows only admin users to access a delete endpoint

promptTemplates.title: User extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Extract user email and roles from JWT payload and return in response with User from_payload

bestPractices: Set short token expiration (15-30 minutes) and implement refresh token flow for longer sessions
Never store passwords, PII, or secrets in JWT claims - keep payloads small and public
Always verify token expiration and implement token blacklist for logout functionality
antiPatterns: Using algorithm='none' - always use HS256 or RS256 for production
Storing sensitive data in JWT tokens - anyone can decode the payload
Skipping expiration verification - expired tokens should be rejected immediately

faq.question: Which Python frameworks work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill targets FastAPI and general Python projects. Other frameworks like Django require different patterns.

faq.question: What JWT algorithms are supported?
faq.answer: HS256 (HMAC-SHA256) is recommended for symmetric key setups. RS256 support requires additional key handling.

faq.question: How do I integrate with frontend authentication?
faq.answer: Use the separate auth-integration skill for React and Next.js. Backend handles verification, frontend handles storage.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for production use?
faq.answer: Yes - follow the security checklist: use strong secrets (32+ chars), short expiry, and implement token blacklisting.

faq.question: Why are my tokens being rejected?
faq.answer: Common causes: expired token, wrong secret key, missing Bearer prefix, or clock skew between servers.

faq.question: How does this compare to session-based auth?
faq.answer: JWT is stateless and scalable for APIs. Sessions store state server-side but work better for traditional web apps.
